page_content:
PROJECT MANAGEMENT MINOR

Civil and Environmental Engineering (ENCE)

1173 Engineering Classroom Building

Phone: 301-405-7768

http://pm.umd.edu/program/undergraduate-minor-in-project- management/

Program Director: Qingbin Cui, Ph.D.

The Minor in Project Management is open to all students in the A.J. Clark School of Engineering or the School of Architecture, Planning & Preservation that have earned at least 60 credit hours and have a GPA of 3.0 or higher.

A basic understanding of project management is becoming increasingly important for engineers. Such knowledge enables them to contribute immediately to employers, and to advance their careers. In addition to a strong engineering background, there is signiﬁcant need for engineers to understand the fundamentals of managing projects in order to effectively participate as members of project teams. Students who successfully complete minor requirements will receive a notation on their ofﬁcial transcript.
